description_long: This bonsai features a thick, gnarled trunk with multiple prominent branches radiating outward, giving it a robust and ancient appearance. The bark is textured and rugged, with a natural grayish-brown coloration that enhances its realistic look. Sparse clusters of small, green leaves are distributed along the branches, creating a balanced yet open canopy. The foliage is denser near the branch tips, while the inner sections reveal more of the intricate wood structure. The base of the trunk is wide and solid, suggesting stability and age. There is no visible pot, and the roots appear to merge directly with the ground or display surface.
description: A robust bonsai with a thick, gnarled trunk and sparse clusters of green leaves on radiating branches. The bark is rugged and grayish-brown, with a wide, stable base.
description_view_0: The front view highlights the thick, multi-branched trunk and the spread of foliage, with the base and main structure clearly visible.
description_view_1: This angled view emphasizes the radial spread of the branches and the distribution of leaves, showing more of the canopy's depth.
description_view_2: From above, the dense branching pattern and the open spaces between foliage clusters are most apparent.
description_view_3: This side view showcases the trunk's curvature and the way branches extend outward, with the foliage appearing slightly denser on the right.
attribute_pot_type: No visible pot; the trunk appears to merge directly with the ground or display surface.
attribute_trunk_curvature: The trunk is thick, rugged, and features multiple prominent curves and splits, following an informal upright style with natural bends and movement.
attribute_branch_arrangement: Branches radiate outward from the trunk in a somewhat irregular, natural pattern, with moderate spacing and a balanced silhouette.
attribute_leaf_density: The foliage is moderately dense, with clusters of leaves concentrated at the branch tips and more open areas near the trunk.
